title: chore(polli): drop alpha, cut 0.1.0, harden publish CI
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Polli CLI graduates
gist.blurb: The Pollinations CLI has officially dropped its alpha tags and hit 0.1.0. We also fixed the publishing robots so they stop silently failing when we forget to bump versions.
gist.summary: Cut the stable 0.1.0 release for the Pollinations CLI and updated the GitHub Actions workflow to handle version publishing more gracefully.
gist.impact: Running npm install for the CLI now correctly pulls the latest stable version instead of getting stuck on old alphas.
gist.visual_concept: The Pollinations bee (Polli) wearing a tiny graduation cap, holding a shiny new software package or floppy disk. A chunky retro terminal in the background showing a successful npm install.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny graduation cap, proudly holding a shiny pixelated software box labeled '0.1.0'. A chunky retro computer terminal in the background glows with lime green (#ecf874) text showing a successful installation. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
